description: Sample cable responses. Quantities for sampling include cable element force, stress and yield state, and shear coupling spring displacement, stress and slip state. If a cable is created using the structure cable create , then the nodes of each element so created will be ordered such that the overall cable direction goes from the begin point to the end point (i.e., the nodal connectivity of each element will be ordered such that the direction from end-1 to end-2 corresponds with the direction from the begin point to the end point).

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: average axial force in the element (positive: tension; negative: compression)

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: average axial stress in the element (positive: tension; negative: compression)

keywords.name: yield-tension
keywords.description: tensile yield state of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: yield-compression
keywords.description: compressive yield state of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: grout-displacement
keywords.description: displacement in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Sign convention is +/- with respect to the average axial direction of cable.

keywords.name: grout-slip
keywords.description: slip state in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never slipped, now slipping, or slipped in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: grout-stress
keywords.description: stress in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Sign convention is +/- with respect to the average axial direction of cable.

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the component ID number. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: the quantity will be sampled at end i (where i is 1 or 2 ), which corresponds with the order in the nodal-connectivity list. The history is sampled at end 1 (the default) if this keyword is not supplied.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by ( v ) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.
